evals.prompt: Please turn my lecture notes into a study guide. I'm in 10th-grade biology and have an exam on cell division. [Attached: ~1500-word typed lecture notes on mitosis covering prophase, metaphase, anaphase, telophase, cytokinesis, chromatids, centromere, and the spindle apparatus, with bolded key terms.]

Here are my econ lecture slides on supply and demand (25 slides, PDF). Make me a study guide for the midterm. I'm in intro microeconomics, college level. The deck covers market definition, law of supply, law of demand, shifts, equilibrium price, surplus/shortage, elasticity, price ceilings/floors, taxes/subsidies, and a numeric worked example (Qd = 100 − 2P, Qs = 20 + 2P).

I have a plaintext transcript from my CS lecture on dynamic programming (no speaker labels, ~3000 words, with timestamps and auto-caption fragmentation and filler words like 'um' and 'you know'). Clean it up and make me a study guide — I need to review for tomorrow. College-level CS course. Content covers memoization, tabulation, Fibonacci, longest common subsequence, and 0/1 knapsack.

Here are my intro-psych lecture notes from weeks 1, 2, and 3 (three separate markdown files). Combine them into one study guide for the first exam. College level. Week 1: history and schools of psychology. Week 2: research methods. Week 3: biological bases (neurons, synapse, neurotransmitters).

Here are my linear algebra notes on eigenvalues and eigenvectors. I'm in a graduate-level class — make me a dense study guide. Notes cover Av = λv, characteristic polynomial det(A − λI) = 0, a 2×2 worked example, diagonalization, and the spectral theorem for symmetric matrices.
